title: Structured output from LLM + prompt tuning
steps: Design structured response schema: phase (enum: context|brainstorm|structure|refine), messageType (question|checklist|slide_content|tip|summary), message (markdown string), suggestions[] (2-3 quick-reply options), slides[] (full deck per message in structure/refine), qualityScore (after outline generation).

Rework streaming architecture — dual-mode: listen for both 'text' events (stream to client) and 'inputJson' events (accumulate metadata silently). On 'end', parse tool input JSON, save text + metadata to DB, send final metadata payload to client via SSE.

Prompt rewrite — collaborative tone, less repetitive questioning, loosen rigid problem framing, keep audience + concrete example patterns. Add tool-use instructions so model always produces text block first then calls tool for metadata.
DB migration — add nullable metadata jsonb column to messages table. Update schema.ts, storage layer (addMessageToConversation, getConversation mapping), and Message type in types.ts.
Frontend SSE parser — update streamMsg() to handle new 'metadata' event type that arrives after text streaming completes.
Fallback handling — if model doesn't use the tool (stop_reason != tool_use), treat as plain text with no metadata (today's behavior). Monitor for max_tokens truncation.
acceptance_criteria: Haiku 4.5 responds with both streamed text AND structured metadata via tool_use
Text still streams word-by-word to the client (no UX regression)
Metadata (phase, messageType, suggestions, slides, qualityScore) stored in DB
Frontend receives metadata after stream completes
Backward compat: old conversations with null metadata still load and render
Prompt feels collaborative, not interrogative — fewer repetitive questions
Suggestions array populated with 2-3 clickable options per response

risks.detail: stream.on('text') is blind to tool_use content blocks. Must listen for both 'text' and 'inputJson' events. Model must be instructed to produce text block first, then tool_use block.
risks.mitigation: Dual-mode streaming: text events stream to client, inputJson events accumulate silently. Parse on 'end' event.

risks.detail: Structured JSON + text in one response can exceed 2048 tokens, especially in structure/refine phases with full slide decks.
risks.mitigation: Bump max_tokens to 4096 or set dynamically per phase. Monitor stop_reason: 'max_tokens'. Keep slide content tight (titles + bullets only).

risks.detail: Tool definition adds ~200-400 tokens per request. But tool definitions are cacheable with prompt caching, so marginal cost is minimal after first request.

context.user_feedback_driving_changes: Users reported the bot feels like an interrogation, not a collaboration. Wants suggestion buttons, markdown formatting, punchier flow. Prompt tuning folded into this task.
context.key_technical_decision: Dual-mode streaming — text streams progressively, tool_use metadata arrives at end. Full slide deck per message (not incremental diffs) to avoid state reconstruction bugs.
